ip_outcome_note: Card name explicitly reads 'Baby // Rampaging Great Ape Baby'. Baby is the primary antagonist of Dragon Ball GT's Baby Saga — a Tuffle parasite with Machine Mutant traits. Card type line confirms Machine Mutant trait. Identity is unambiguous: the card name IS the character name.

trivia_bullets.text: BT4-002 is a double-sided Leader Card — the standard flip-leader format for the DBS TCG. The front (Baby) awakens when life drops to 4 or fewer, flipping to Rampaging Great Ape Baby and granting a power-reduction effect on every attack. [Source: WebSearch snippet via DBZ Exchange / dbs-cardgame.com strategy page]

Colossal Warfare (DBS-B04) released July 13, 2018 as the final set of the Over Realm block — the largest DBS booster to that point at 218 cards. Red in BT4 is explicitly centered on the Baby/Machine Mutant strategy per Bandai's own strategy notes: 'Red in series 4 is mostly focused on Great Ape Baby.' [Source: dbs-cardgame.com strategy/series4/red.php]

Baby is the Machine Mutant tribe leader in BT4. His Leader ability reduces opponent Battle Card power by -10000 per attack on the back side, creating a persistent board erosion strategy that pairs with BT4-018 Baby, Vengeance Unleashed (Union-Absorb, 1-cost tutor for Baby cards). [Source: WebSearch snippets via dbs-cardgame.com, DBZ Exchange]

Baby's character is the last survivor of the Tuffles — a race exterminated by Saiyans on Planet Vegeta before the Dragon Ball Z timeline. His revenge arc in Dragon Ball GT eventually sees him possess Vegeta as a permanent host, infecting most of Earth. The creation paradox: Baby created Dr. Myuu (his own creator) in a bootstrap loop. [Source: Wikipedia: Dragon Ball GT; CBR article 'Dragon Ball GT Baby vs. Dr. Myuu True Creator']
sources: CBR.com: Dragon Ball GT's Baby Created Dr. Myuu Oddity
